required_complexity_patterns.notes: Combobox: button or input with aria-haspopup=listbox plus listbox/options
Field labeled via aria-labelledby pointing at hidden or distant span
Label wraps input through nested divs without for attribute
Apply form hosted inside iframe or srcdoc iframe shell
Inputs laid out in table cells, not flex card stacks only

prompt_summary: Create a messy, production-real ashby-style job application (single-page, small, tier=high). Widgets: combobox. Required: Combobox: button or input with aria-haspopup=listbox plus listbox/options Required: Field labeled via aria-labelledby pointing at hidden or distant span Required: Label wraps input through nested divs without for attribute Required: Apply form hosted inside iframe or srcdoc iframe shell Required: Inputs laid out in table cells, not flex card stacks only Reference DOM (Definition list form): Fields structured as dl/dt/dd definition list pairs Reference DOM (Triple nested fieldsets): Fieldset inside fieldset inside fieldset with legends at each level Reference DOM (Collapsible section animation): Additional questions in collapsible section toggled by aria-expanded button Do NOT produce a tutorial-style form where every field is label[for]+native input. Mix native controls with non-semantic production DOM from the patterns below. Implement ALL required complexity patterns: custom-combobox, aria-labelledby-hidden, label-wraps-deep, iframe-apply, table-layout. Include at least one custom widget (div role=textbox, pill buttons, or listbox combobox). Include at least one labeling strategy besides label-for (aria-label, aria-labelledby, glued spans, placeholder-only). Layout: fieldset stacks with legend-only section titles. Avoid generic centered SaaS card as the only structure. High tier: include shadow DOM host OR iframe apply shell OR wizard/conditional reveal with hidden fields. Mirror structural messiness like these references: Definition list form; Triple nested fieldsets; Collapsible section animation.
